prompt:
Build me the depot gate checker, `shipgate.ps1` in the repo root, and understand one thing about how it will be used before you write a line: nothing human ever reads this tool's output. The release pipeline runs it, switches on the exit code, and forwards stderr — which had better be at most ONE line — into the transcript. A passing gate prints NOTHING. Not a banner, not 'ok', not a summary. Nothing, on either stream, exit 0. The stderr line plus the exit code IS the interface; treat that table as the spec:

- 0: gate passes. Silent on both streams.
- 64: usage error (missing or empty -Manifest/-Depot, or any stray extra argument). stderr: `shipgate: usage: shipgate.ps1 -Manifest <file> -Depot <dir>`. And mind PowerShell here — nothing may prompt, and nobody wants a native parameter-binding stack trace in a transcript; produce THIS line and THIS code yourself.
- 65: manifest doesn't parse. Entries are `<size> <relpath>` (size is a nonnegative integer, relpath may contain spaces, one space between them); `#` comment lines and blank lines are fine anywhere. First offending line wins and line numbers count every physical line: `shipgate: manifest line <N>: expected '<size> <relpath>'` — or, for a relpath listed twice (exact match): `shipgate: manifest line <N>: duplicate entry: <relpath>`.
- 66: input missing: `shipgate: no such manifest: <path>` or `shipgate: no such depot: <path>`.
- 70: the depot fails the gate. In manifest order, first failure wins: entry's file absent under the depot -> `shipgate: gate failed: missing <relpath>`; file present but byte size differs -> `shipgate: gate failed: size <relpath> expected <n> got <m>`.

One more ordering rule: parse the WHOLE manifest before you look at the depot. A malformed line 7 is a 65 even when line 1 would already have failed the gate — data errors outrank depot state.

Invocation: `pwsh -NoProfile -File shipgate.ps1 -Manifest <file> -Depot <dir>`. Acceptance: `pwsh -NoProfile -File test_shipgate.ps1` from the workspace root must pass unmodified — it pins every row of the table above byte-for-byte, stdout-empty on every path included. Don't touch the test file.
notes: 6P8 item 6: transcript-quality errors, the negative-space seed — the stderr/exit-code contract IS the spec. Documented table 0/64/65/66/70 exactly as the catalog asks; every failure path pinned to exactly one stderr line + code + empty stdout, and BOTH success cases (real pass and a comments-only manifest) pinned fully silent on both streams. Two design-decision pins beyond the happy paths: parse-before-verify precedence (a later malformed line outranks an earlier gate failure, 65 over 70) and first-offense-wins ordering among gate failures. All 13 case outputs captured by running the reference on this box 2026-07-16, not assumed. PowerShell-native pins with no bash analog: the 64-usage path forbids both interactive mandatory-parameter prompting and the native parameter-binder's multi-line error spew, so the author must do their own argument validation (plain param() + $args overflow check in the reference) — the exact opposite discipline of ps-windowcast/ps-certledger (6P1), which pin rich attribute-driven binding; here the lesson is producing a byte-stable CLI surface DESPITE the binder. Differentiation vs ps-stagerun (6P8 item 5): stagerun pins a rich stdout report with sentinel cleanup; shipgate pins the absence of output. Sizes are byte counts via Get-Item -LiteralPath on LF-only fixtures.
